content_of_post_to_answer: Why does my skin seem to react whenever life gets overwhelming?
answer: Skin reactions under overwhelm are often less about ‘triggers’ and more about the body reallocating attention. When mental load exceeds what the nervous system can process cleanly, it shifts resources toward immediate survival physiology—sleep, inflammation, barrier repair lose priority. So the skin isn’t randomly reacting; it’s one of the first places the system shows overload. It’s visible accounting of internal bandwidth.
